name: Apache Kafka / Confluent Cloud API
what_it_does: Apache Kafka event streaming platform (via Confluent Cloud REST API) for high-throughput, durable, replayable message streaming and event-driven architectures at scale.
use_cases: High-throughput event ingestion from IoT, clickstream, or application events
Event sourcing and CQRS patterns for distributed systems
Real-time data pipelines between systems with schema evolution
Change data capture (CDC) from databases for downstream consumers
Agent event bus for distributed AI workflow coordination
not_for: Simple task queuing with few messages (SQS or RabbitMQ are simpler)
best_when: You need durable, replayable event streams at high throughput with multiple independent consumers reading the same events.
avoid_when: You need simple work queues, don't need replay semantics, or can't justify Kafka's operational complexity.

auth.notes: Confluent Cloud uses API keys (Kafka API key/secret) for native protocol access. REST API uses HTTP Basic Auth with API key/secret. OAuth2 available for Confluent Platform enterprise. SASL/PLAIN or SASL/SCRAM for self-hosted.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $400 free credits for new Confluent Cloud accounts
pricing.paid_tiers: Basic clusters from $0.11/GB data; Standard/Dedicated for production
pricing.notes: Apache Kafka is free and open-source for self-hosting. Confluent Cloud managed service has usage-based pricing. MSK (AWS) and Event Hubs (Azure) offer Kafka-compatible managed services.

performance.rate_limits: Millions of messages/second with appropriate cluster sizing

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Kafka protocol errors are numeric codes. Confluent REST Proxy returns JSON errors. Error handling in client libraries is well-documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Kafka producers support idempotent delivery (exactly-once semantics) with producer ID and sequence numbers. Transactions available for atomic multi-partition writes.
known_agent_gotchas: Kafka native protocol is TCP binary — agents need Kafka client libraries, not simple HTTP
Confluent REST Proxy adds HTTP interface but adds latency overhead vs native clients
Consumer group management is critical — agents in same group compete for partitions, different groups get independent copies
Topic partitioning affects parallelism — plan partition count before production (hard to change)
Schema Registry required for Avro/Protobuf schemas — adds another service to manage
Retention period settings determine how long messages are replayable — set thoughtfully
